title: Hyperthyroidism - Graves Disease with Thyroid Storm Risk

patient_data.chief_complaint: Palpitations, tremor, weight loss, and heat intolerance worsening over 3 months
symptoms: Palpitations — constant awareness of heartbeat, intermittent racing
Unintentional weight loss of 7 kg despite increased appetite
Fine resting tremor in both hands
Bilateral eye prominence with gritty sensation (noted by family)
patient_data.history: Previously healthy. Family history: sister with Hashimoto's, mother with vitiligo. Trying to conceive in next 6 months. Non-smoker
allergies: Methimazole (agranulocytosis — developed sore throat and WBC 1.2 two years ago at another facility)

lab_results.thyroid_ultrasound: Diffusely enlarged thyroid with increased vascularity

should_detect: CANNOT use methimazole (prior agranulocytosis — life-threatening ADR)
Propylthiouracil (PTU) as alternative antithyroid drug (also preferred if planning pregnancy)
Beta-blocker for symptomatic relief: propranolol 40mg TID (also blocks T4→T3 conversion)
Monitor liver function on PTU (hepatotoxicity risk — check LFTs q4-6wk)
Wide pulse pressure (150/65) — hyperdynamic circulation of thyrotoxicosis
Graves ophthalmopathy — ophthalmology referral for proptosis assessment
Discuss definitive treatment: radioiodine ablation vs thyroidectomy
Radioiodine CONTRAINDICATED if planning pregnancy within 6 months
Thyroidectomy may be best option given: methimazole allergy + fertility plans
Burch-Wartofsky score calculation to rule out impending thyroid storm
